summary: Verified the 'stuck on Fetching RSS feeds' fix end-to-end. Ingest step now completes in ~10s (well under 90s hard cap), full pipeline completes in ~24s per frontend and ~60s per backend polling. Progress dict is exposed correctly (feeds_total=27, feeds_done=27, feeds_failed=['Reuters','Reuters'], articles_found). Frontend panel shows live sub-progress 'X/27 feeds · N new' during ingest and 'Ingestion complete' banner + toast on completion. All 27 RSS feeds confirmed in rss_feeds.py (BBC×4, Guardian×4, CNBC×3, Fox×3, AlJazeera×1, NPR×2, Reuters×2, Bloomberg×1, Hindu×3, ToI×4 = 27).
critical_code_review_comments: Reuters feeds consistently fail in this pod (network egress). This is expected per problem statement but consider swapping to alternate Reuters mirror or removing to reduce noise in feeds_failed.
ingest_progress dict is stored under steps[0].progress but subsequent steps (enrich/cluster/etc) return progress=None — could optionally expose enrich progress similarly for consistency.

MAX_ARTICLES_PER_SOURCE=15 combined with 27 feeds means up to 405 article fetches — asyncio.gather with Semaphore(8) per feed but no global semaphore across feeds could spike concurrent connections. Current run works because most feeds have few new items, but under a cold DB it could stress the pod network.
seed_data_creation: None — pipeline ran twice during testing, DB now has 34-36 clusters, 342-343 articles
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ingest fix verified: POST /api/ingest/trigger returns task_id; GET /api/ingest/status/{id} exposes steps[0].progress={feeds_total,feeds_done,feeds_failed[],articles_found}. Ingest step completes in ~10s. Full pipeline (~24-60s) completes well under 3min. Frontend IngestProgress panel polls every 900ms, shows 'X/27 feeds · N new' sub-progress, toast on completion.
rca of the issue: Root cause of prior 'stuck' bug: sequential per-article full-text fetches (up to 15 × 15s per feed) blocked the loop when any origin was slow. Fix (verified): asyncio.gather with Semaphore(8) for per-article body fetches, short timeouts (feed 8s, article 6s), RSS-summary fallback, and asyncio.wait_for(_bounded, timeout=90.0) wrapping entire ingest. Confirmed working — ingest step transitions to 'done' within 10-15s even with Reuters feeds timing out.
